statement: Temporal code should live under packages/engine but workers should run under Node, not Bun, with a local dev Temporal server and a production self-hosted service.
rationale: Temporal's TypeScript worker runtime is Node-specific, while colocating the code in packages/engine preserves repo ergonomics and ownership.
keyAssumptions: Bun can remain the general repo tool while Node is used for Temporal workers
The team accepts a split runtime model
validationCriteria: A worker can boot locally from packages/engine and connect to a local Temporal service
The repo build/test flow can include a Node-only Temporal worker entrypoint cleanly
critiquePoints: Mixed runtime setup increases developer environment complexity
questions: Should prod use a single worker process with multiple task queues or multiple worker services?
